title: Leaning Wall Shelf
summary: A ladder-style shelf that leans against the wall on long angled side rails, with five plywood shelves that get shallower as they climb and a picture-hanger anchor at the top.
description:
Two long 1x4 side rails run the full height of the piece at a lean, and five plywood shelves step between them — each one shallower than the last, so the whole thing narrows as it climbs and reads as a single sloped ladder rather than a stack of even boxes. Cut and mark both side rails together in the first step, since the shelf-support positions have to land identically on each rail or the shelves won't sit level.

**Each shelf gets a 1x4 nosing trim on its two long front-facing edges**, sized to match that shelf's own depth — the top shelf is shallow and gets short trim, the bottom shelf is deep and gets long trim, so don't assume all ten trim pieces are interchangeable. A fifth, longer 1x4 runs behind each shelf where it meets the side rails, tying the ladder together at every level.

**This shelf leans, it doesn't stand free** — it must be anchored to the wall. Attach through the back trim into a wall stud with heavy-duty picture hanging hardware; if a stud doesn't land where you need it, use rated drywall anchors instead. Never rely on the lean alone to keep it upright.

cutList.note: Mark and cut both together — shelf-support positions must match.

steps.title: Mark and cut the side rails
steps.body: Cut both side rails to length together and mark the shelf-support positions identically on each — small mismatches here throw off every shelf above.

steps.title: Attach the shelf supports
steps.body: Pocket-hole and screw the five shelf supports between the side rails at the marked positions, checking each for level as you go.

steps.title: Add the plywood shelves
steps.body: Set each plywood shelf on its support, shallowest at the top, deepest at the bottom, and fasten with self-tapping screws from below.

steps.title: Trim the shelf edges
steps.body: Nail and glue the front-edge trim to each shelf, matching trim length to that shelf's own depth, then attach the back trim at each level tying into the side rails.

steps.title: Finish and anchor to the wall
steps.body: Fill nail holes, sand, and finish as desired. Anchor the back trim into a wall stud with heavy-duty picture hanging hardware, or rated drywall anchors if no stud lands in position — this piece must not be left freestanding.

unresolvedImages.alt: Leaning wall shelf with five plywood shelves
